paper.title: The high velocity stars in the Local Stellar Halo from Gaia and LAMOST

extraction.summary: 3 unbound high-velocity stars (HiVel14, HiVel15, HiVel16) from a sample of 16 HiVel stars identified from 436 kinematically-selected local halo stars from Gaia DR1 (TGAS) + LAMOST DR4. The paper explicitly states: '3 HiVel stars (HiVel14, HiVel15 and HiVel16) are unbound due to their very high velocity.' These are FGK-type stars with V_gsr = 644, 753, 734 km/s respectively. The remaining 13 HiVel stars have bound orbits. The paper focuses on halo kinematics and chemistry, not primarily HVS discovery. Gaia DR1 (TGAS) source IDs are provided in Table 2. No Gaia DR2 data used.

method_chain.summary: Cross-matched Gaia DR1 (TGAS) with LAMOST DR4. TGAS provides 5-parameter astrometry (positions, parallaxes, proper motions) for ~2 million Tycho-2 stars. LAMOST DR4 provides radial velocities, atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H], [alpha/Fe]) for ~4.33 million AFGK-type stars.
outputs: Cross-matched catalog with 6D phase space + chemistry

source_refs.context: Gaia DR1 provides 5D measurements...radial velocity and metallicity from LAMOST

method_chain.summary: Quality cuts: Teff 3500-8000 K, log g > 0.5, [Fe/H] > -4.0. Halo stars selected kinematically with relative speed >= 220 km/s w.r.t. LSR (Toomre diagram). 436 halo stars identified.

source_refs.context: The halo stars are identified kinematically with a relative speed of at least 220 km/s with respect to the local standard of rest. In total, 436 halo stars are identified.

method_chain.summary: HiVel star selection: absolute Galactic radial velocity > 200 km/s, proper motion and distance relative errors < 50%, Galactic rest frame velocity V_gsr > 300 km/s. 16 HiVel stars identified.

source_refs.context: We then selected the HiVel stars with absolute Galactic radial velocity distribution greater than 200 km/s...V_gsr>300 km/s gave us a HiVel candidate sample containing 16 stars.

method_chain.summary: Space velocities (U,V,W) computed from TGAS proper motions, LAMOST radial velocities, and parallax distances. Velocities transformed to Galactic rest frame V_gsr using solar motion and LSR corrections.
outputs: U, V, W, V_gsr for 16 HiVel stars (Table 3)

source_refs.context: Table 3: Spatial positions and Velocities of 16 HiVel stars

method_chain.summary: Backward orbit integration for 1 Gyr using McMillan (2017) Galactic potential model (cold gas discs, thin/thick stellar discs, bulge, dark matter halo). Stars integrated backward from current positions with negative velocities.
inputs: Current positions and velocities of HiVel stars
outputs: Orbital parameters (e, Zmax) for 13 bound HiVel stars; 3 unbound could not be integrated

source_refs.context: We use a recent Galactic potential model provided in McMillan (2017)
3 HiVel stars (HiVel14, HiVel15 and HiVel16) are unbound due to their very high velocity and only the orbit of 13 HiVel stars could be determined.

method_chain.summary: Origin assessment based on backward orbits and chemical abundances. HiVel4,9,10 likely from disrupted dwarf galaxy/globular cluster; HiVel7 from thick disk; HiVel5,13,3,8 possibly from near GC; HiVel1,2,11,12 from Galactic disk.

source_refs.context: HiVel4, HiVel9 and HiVel10 could originate from the tidal debris of an accreted and disrupted dwarf galaxy or globular cluster
Table 2: HiVel14 source-id 3817216883707348352
Table 3: HiVel14 V_gsr=644 km/s

inclusion_assessment.summary: HiVel14: TGAS source-id 3817216883707348352, Teff~3680 K, log g~1.83, [Fe/H]~-0.32. V_gsr=644 km/s. Explicitly identified as unbound by the paper. Cool, low-gravity star, possibly a giant. Located at l=249.93, b=58.72.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states '3 HiVel stars (HiVel14, HiVel15 and HiVel16) are unbound due to their very high velocity'. Uses V_gsr > 300 km/s threshold and McMillan+2017 potential for orbit integration.

source_refs.context: In all 16 HiVel stars, 3 HiVel stars (HiVel14, HiVel15 and HiVel16) are unbound due to their very high velocity

16 HiVel stars selected from 436 halo stars with V_gsr>300 km/s

inclusion_assessment.summary: HiVel15: TGAS source-id 645807259905057664, Teff~3694 K, log g~1.83, [Fe/H]~-0.32. V_gsr=753 km/s (highest in sample). Explicitly unbound. Similar atmospheric parameters to HiVel14, possibly a binary or related object. l=203.27, b=45.64.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Explicitly stated as unbound with V_gsr=753 km/s, well above expected Galactic escape velocity.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HiVel16: TGAS source-id 2086860081541233408, Teff~5733 K, log g~4.36, [Fe/H]~-0.35. V_gsr=734 km/s. Explicitly unbound. Solar-type dwarf. l=82.37, b=10.25.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Explicitly stated as unbound with V_gsr=734 km/s.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 13 bound HiVel stars (HiVel1-13) with V_gsr > 300 km/s but bound in the McMillan+2017 Galactic potential. These are halo high-velocity stars that do not exceed escape velocity. Orbits were computed backward for 1 Gyr.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Not HVS candidates. These 13 HiVel stars have V_gsr > 300 km/s but are bound in the McMillan+2017 potential. They are halo high-velocity stars, not unbound hypervelocity stars. The paper explicitly identifies only HiVel14, HiVel15, and HiVel16 as unbound.

source_refs.context: In all 16 HiVel stars, 3 HiVel stars (HiVel14, HiVel15 and HiVel16) are unbound due to their very high velocity and only the orbit of 13 HiVel stars could be determined.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 420 remaining kinematically-selected halo stars (436 total minus 16 HiVel) with relative speed >= 220 km/s w.r.t. LSR.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Not HVS candidates. These are kinematically-selected halo stars with V_LSR >= 220 km/s, all below the HiVel threshold of V_gsr > 300 km/s. The paper studies their metallicity distribution and orbital properties for halo population studies.

source_refs.context: 436 halo stars are identified...From this halo sample, 16 high velocity (HiVel) stars are identified.
